province.note: Located in Ban Thi, Lamphun. Commonly marketed under Chiang Mai region.

par.note: Course features a par-6 hole (666 yards) — confirm total par with official source.

year_opened.note: Originally opened 2006 as Gassan Marina Golf Club. Renamed and redesigned as Gassan Panorama Golf Club in 2017.

green_fee_weekday_thb.note: No weekday/weekend distinction — same rate. May be low-season rate; high-season may be higher.

caddie_fee_thb.note: Included in compulsory package per most booking sources. Separate fee TBC.

cart_fee_thb.note: Included in compulsory package. Separate fee TBC.

phone.note: Gassan Group shared number — confirm direct Panorama number.

address.value: 99 Moo 17, Makua Jae, Ban Thi, Lamphun 51180, Thailand

latitude.note: Approximate — Ban Thi, Lamphun. Needs map verification.

drive_time_from_bangkok_min.note: Lamphun/Chiang Mai area is ~700km from Bangkok — visitors fly (1h10m BKK→CNX), drive not practical

prose.overview: Gassan Panorama Golf Club is an 18-hole championship course in Ban Thi, Lamphun province, redesigned and relaunched in 2017 as the third and most architecturally ambitious property in the Gassan Golf Group. Originally opened in 2006 as Gassan Marina Golf Club, the course was closed for a comprehensive redesign by Thai architect Pravit Reang-Po that transformed it into one of the longest golf courses in Thailand, stretching to 7,761 yards from the championship tees. The layout is built around a large central lake — all 18 holes were designed with views of or interaction with this water feature — giving the course a dramatic, open aspect quite different from the forested mountain settings of Gassan Khuntan. The clubhouse architecture blends European and Middle Eastern influences, providing an unexpectedly grand entrance for what is otherwise a Thai countryside setting. Gassan Panorama appeals to low-handicap players who want maximum yardage and a visually spectacular water-dominated layout, while the mountain skyline backdrop adds the Northern Thai scenic character that makes the region distinct.
prose.layout_and_experience:
The routing places the central lake as the defining element of nearly every hole. Par-3s regularly demand full carries over open water, and approach shots to many of the par-4s and par-5s must navigate lake margins. The course's signature feature is an unusually long par hole — reportedly a par-6 measuring approximately 666 yards — that gives the layout its exceptional total yardage and a strategic challenge absent from most Thai championship courses.

At 7,761 yards, Gassan Panorama is not well-suited to high-handicap players from back tees; the forward tee positions reduce the layout to a more manageable 7,072 yards, and the course plays differently depending on the combination of tees selected. The fairways are generous in width, encouraging big drives, but the lake edges punish anything that drifts off line on the approach. Several par-3s carry over the full width of the lake — these require confident club selection and provide some of the most photographed moments in Northern Thai golf.

The greens were built to be large and slightly elevated, providing receptive targets on long approach shots but with subtle contours that reward precise putting. The course plays best in the cool dry season (November–February), when firm fairways extend drives and the mountain scenery around the lake is at its clearest.
prose.tips: Confirm the current green fee before booking — the 1,400 THB rate is the base starting price and all-in packages including caddie and cart are typically offered at higher rates. High-season packages (November–March) from online booking platforms typically bundle green fee, caddie, and cart into a single rate. Caddie service is compulsory and strongly recommended on a layout where club selection over water is critical. Play from the appropriate tee for your handicap — the back tees demand a long, accurate game and will test even strong players. Morning tee times are recommended during the hot season (March–May) to finish before peak afternoon temperatures. The club rental rate is approximately 1,400 THB for a set — confirm at the pro shop on arrival.
prose.location_and_access: Gassan Panorama Golf Club is located in Ban Thi, Lamphun province, approximately 25 minutes south of Chiang Mai International Airport (CNX) — the Gassan Golf Group markets it as the closest of their three properties to the airport. Visitors from Bangkok fly to CNX (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes from Suvarnabhumi or Don Mueang) — driving the 700 kilometres from Bangkok is not practical for a golf trip. The course is accessible by Grab taxi or private car from either Chiang Mai city or the airport; the Gassan Group can arrange transfers on request.
prose.rental_cta_context: Heading to Gassan Panorama Golf Club for a round on one of Northern Thailand's longest courses? Rent premium clubs in Bangkok — delivered to your hotel before you fly — so you arrive in Chiang Mai ready to play without airline baggage fees.
